nodes.norm_label: { app, browserwindow, ipcmain, dialog, menu, tray, shell, nativeimage }

nodes.label: Adaptive T — Dynamic timing for Premiere ExtendScript commands
nodes.rationale: adaptiveT measures elapsed ms per successful Premiere API call and uses that as the wait before the next command. This prevents hard-coded delays from being too short (causes parent-not-found errors) or too long (wastes user time). Minimum floors of 250ms (bins) and 30ms (sequences) guard against extremely fast hardware.
nodes.norm_label: adaptive t — dynamic timing for premiere extendscript commands

nodes.label: Inline IIFE ExtendScript pattern (never call named hostscript.jsx functions)
nodes.rationale: Named hostscript.jsx functions silently fail in CEP when called via evalScript. All ExtendScript code in ext.js is therefore inlined as IIFEs to guarantee execution. Fixed in v1.8.3.
nodes.norm_label: inline iife extendscript pattern (never call named hostscript.jsx functions)

nodes.label: Electron Context Isolation + IPC Security Bridge
nodes.rationale: contextIsolation=true prevents renderer from accessing Node APIs directly. All renderer-main communication must go through window.api exposed by contextBridge in preload.js.
nodes.norm_label: electron context isolation + ipc security bridge

nodes.label: SQLite In-place Schema Migration Pattern
nodes.rationale: All migration DDL is wrapped in try-catch so a failed migration degrades gracefully instead of crashing the app on startup. Uses ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN where possible, and table recreation for NOT NULL constraint changes (SQLite limitation).

nodes.label: Smart Project Metadata Tagging & Client Profiles

nodes.label: freeXan Project Context for Claude Code
nodes.author: Swastik / BloomX
nodes.norm_label: freexan project context for claude code

nodes.label: IPC Security Bridge Pattern (preload.js window.api)
nodes.rationale: Context isolation is enabled; all renderer-to-main communication goes through the preload.js window.api bridge to prevent direct Node.js access from renderer processes

nodes.label: Premiere Pro Project Builder Codebase Documentation
nodes.author: Swastik / BloomX
nodes.norm_label: premiere pro project builder codebase documentation

nodes.label: Active Project Detection (PowerShell + CEP fallback)
nodes.rationale: Two-signal approach: PowerShell title polling every 2.5s as primary, CEP WebSocket as fallback/complement, to detect active Premiere project path

nodes.label: Media Import via Overlay Pipeline
nodes.source_location: Key Flows - Media Import via Overlay

nodes.label: WebSocket Protocol Port 4554
nodes.rationale: Port 4554 hardcoded in both main.js and cep-extension/ext.js for WebSocket server/client communication between Electron and Premiere Pro CEP panel

nodes.label: Template Resolution Priority (7-level cascade)
nodes.rationale: 7-level cascade (C,F,T)→(C,F,*)→(C,*,T)→(*,F,T)→(C,*,*)→(*,F,*)→(*,*,T)→default allows assigning a template to any single dimension so it applies to all matching projects lacking a more specific assignment

nodes.label: Log Maintenance Policy (CHANGELOG + DEV_LOG + NAVIGATION_LOG)
nodes.rationale: All three log files must be updated after every code change so that any new person or AI agent can understand the project's current state without reading every source file

nodes.label: Security Rules (no direct Node.js to renderer)
nodes.rationale: Never expose Node.js APIs directly to renderers; use preload.js bridge only; IPC allowlist is the gating mechanism to limit attack surface
nodes.norm_label: security rules (no direct node.js to renderer)

nodes.label: Changelog v2.5.0 — Defensive Audit Fix Pass
nodes.author: Claude Code
nodes.norm_label: changelog v2.5.0 — defensive audit fix pass

nodes.label: ASAR Unpack Native Modules Config
nodes.rationale: better-sqlite3, ffmpeg-installer, cep-extension, and template files must live outside the ASAR archive so Node can load them as regular files from disk at runtime

nodes.label: Invisible Infrastructure Positioning
nodes.rationale: freeXan is positioned as a workflow operating layer, not a simple utility — it should feel invisible, removing friction and automating repetitive actions so editors stay in flow-state
